original_hypothesis.title: De-Whitelisting USDC Version Permanently Locks Depositor Funds
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: ASDUSDC.withdraw() requires whitelistedUSDCVersions[_usdcVersion] to be true. If the owner removes a USDC version from the whitelist via updateWhitelist(_version, false), all depositors who deposited that version can no longer withdraw their specific USDC tokens. Their asdUSDC tokens still exist but can only withdraw other whitelisted versions, which may have insufficient balances.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: There is no state machine constraint preventing de-whitelisting when usdcBalances[version] > 0. The whitelist is used for both deposit AND withdraw, creating a one-way trap if a version is removed.

proof_of_signal.pattern: updateWhitelist has no check for usdcBalances[_usdcVersion] > 0; withdraw requires whitelist check at line 51

preconditions: Users have deposits of that version (usdcBalances > 0)

initial_code_evidence.vulnerable_pattern: No guard preventing de-whitelisting when deposits exist for that version
initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: Users cannot withdraw their specific USDC version after de-whitelisting; USDC tokens locked in contract
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: require(usdcBalances[_usdcVersion] == 0 || !_isWhitelisted == false) in updateWhitelist, or remove whitelist check from withdraw

analysis_questions: Can affected users withdraw using a different whitelisted version?
Does the owner have a migration path for de-whitelisted versions?
Is this an intentional admin capability or oversight?
validation_requirements: Deposit USDC-v1, de-whitelist USDC-v1, attempt withdraw
Verify no alternative withdrawal mechanism exists
Check if owner can re-whitelist to unblock withdrawals

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: Owner whitelists USDC-v1. Users deposit 1M USDC-v1. Owner de-whitelists USDC-v1 (e.g., for migration). 1M USDC-v1 is permanently locked. Users hold asdUSDC but cannot withdraw USDC-v1. Owner can re-whitelist to fix, but the design allows permanent fund lock.
prerequisites: Owner de-whitelists a version with non-zero balance

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: All deposits of the de-whitelisted version locked
potential_impact_if_confirmed.affected_users: All depositors of the de-whitelisted USDC version
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: Owner can re-whitelist the version to unblock

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: No test for withdrawal after de-whitelisting

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
